summary: Kenneth Suna provides foundational personal finance advice for an 18-year-old receiving their first paycheck, focusing on Roth IRA accounts, S&P 500 index funds, and dividend ETFs rather than options trading or premium generation strategies.
takeaways: Build 6-8 month emergency fund before investing
S&P 500 Index Funds historically return 10.5% annually
$250/month invested for 40 years in S&P 500 Index Fund grows to ~$1.9M
Avoid get-rich-quick schemes to prevent losses
Dividend ETFs are suitable for most investors
title: How I Would Invest if I Was 18 Years Old Getting My First Paycheck! #shorts
